inputs.fcf_source: FY2025 operating income $3440.0M plus normalized net investment income contribution, ÷ 534.878M shares = $5.85/sh; bridges GAAP OCF and operating income per share
inputs.normalization_note: FY2025 GAAP net income fell to $3.65B (EPS $6.82) from $5.44B on investment marks; owner cash uses underwriting plus recurring investment spread, not peak 2024 GAAP earnings

bear.notes: Japan persistency pressure, yen headwind, multiple compresses toward mid-teens free cash flow multiple

base.notes: Low-single-digit premium growth in Japan and U.S. worksite channel; investment portfolio supports mid-single-digit owner-cash growth

bull.notes: U.S. supplemental penetration accelerates, Japan third-sector products grow, buybacks continue at premium multiple

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: Insurance assets and liabilities marked through investment portfolio; no historical-cost land floor issue

option_scan.question: Investment portfolio spread not in owner-cash path?
option_scan.evidence: Net investment income $4076.0M in FY2025 revenues; normalized in starting owner cash per share

option_scan.question: Material separate options after scan?
option_scan.evidence: Supplemental insurance compounder; Lawrence full cash-flow path sufficient

growth_explanation.mechanism: Japan cancer and medical supplemental persistency plus U.S. worksite accident and hospital indemnity sales; owner cash grows with earned premium and net investment income on the portfolio.
growth_explanation.source: AFL/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260225_rpt20251231_acc0001628280_26_011402.htm segment revenues; AFL/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-Q_20260506_rpt20260331_acc0001628280_26_030923.htm Q1 2026 premium and investment income trends
growth_explanation.bear_falsifier: Combined benefit ratio rises above 70% for two consecutive years with no offsetting investment income
growth_explanation.bull_falsifier: Japan earned premium grows above 5% for four consecutive quarters while persistency holds

component_valuation.coverage_statement: Four additive components map Japan supplemental engine, U.S. worksite engine, net financial claims, and investment/currency stress reserve once each.

valuation.evidence: Primary bridge from FY2025 10-K: owner cash $3129.0M ($5.85/sh), book $55.13/sh, net investment income $4076.0M, net debt $2164.0M; contract backfill 2026-07-21.
valuation.cross_check: Reconcile to FY2025 10-K and Q1 2026 10-Q before decision use.
valuation.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

inputs.label: Aflac Japan earned premium revenue FY2025

source.locator: Aflac Japan earned premium revenue $10664.0M FY2025

inputs.label: Normalized owner cash (underwriting plus investment spread) FY2025

source.locator: Operating income $3440.0M plus normalized investment contribution; $5.85/sh on 534.878M shares

assumptions.label: Owner cash allocated to Aflac Japan by earned premium share
assumptions.rationale: Japan ~61% of earned premium; aging demographics support third-sector persistency.

assumptions.label: Growth years 1–5 on segment owner cash
assumptions.rationale: Lawrence scenario envelope; Japan aging tailwind or U.S. worksite penetration.

assumptions.label: Growth years 6–7 on segment owner cash
assumptions.rationale: Fade as premium base enlarges or yen translation drags.

assumptions.label: Required return on segment owner cash
assumptions.rationale: Supplemental insurance, investment-mark, and currency risk premium.

assumptions.label: Selling multiple in year 7
assumptions.rationale: Quality supplemental insurer peer multiples on normalized owner cash.

assumptions.label: Component schedule reconciliation factor
assumptions.rationale: Preserves additive component schedule while filing facts anchor owner-cash bridge.

components.label: Net debt and liquidity claims on common equity

valuation.evidence: Primary bridge from FY2025 10-K: owner cash $3129.0M ($5.85/sh), book $55.13/sh, net investment income $4076.0M, net debt $2164.0M; contract backfill 2026-07-21.

inputs.label: Debt and capital lease obligations FY2025

assumptions.label: Net debt claim on common equity (debt less cash, bounded stress band)
assumptions.rationale: Filing-locked net debt $2164.0M ($4.05/sh); low case adds refinancing stress, high case allows modest net cash.

components.label: Investment-mark and yen translation stress reserve

valuation.evidence: Primary bridge from FY2025 10-K: owner cash $3129.0M ($5.85/sh), book $55.13/sh, net investment income $4076.0M, net debt $2164.0M; contract backfill 2026-07-21.
valuation.cross_check: Reconcile to FY2025 10-K and Q1 2026 10-Q before decision use.
valuation.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

source.locator: NetIncomeLoss $3646.0M FY2025 vs $5443M FY2024 on investment marks

assumptions.label: Investment-mark volatility and yen translation stress reserve
assumptions.rationale: Negative reserve for credit spread widening, JGB mark moves, and yen weakness compressing translated U.S. dollar earnings; not full policy reserve subtraction.

trigger.policy: A complete economic-value analysis is required when any trigger fires; otherwise it is recommended.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of Aflac, including Japan and U.S. supplemental owner-cash engines, net financial claims, and investment/currency stress reserve.
economic_claim.unit_source: FY2025 weighted average diluted shares 534.878M per AFL/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260225_rpt20251231_acc0001628280_26_011402.htm.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating segments valued through owner-cash discount paths on premium-proportional owner cash; net debt and stress reserve are separate overlap keys.

economic_value_analysis.accounting_reference: FY2025 10-K: book value per share $55.13; owner cash $5.85/sh; economic value in normalized supplemental underwriting and investment spread.

component_groups.label: Japan supplemental insurance engine
component_groups.economic_claim: Japan cancer and medical supplemental franchise
component_groups.valuation_basis: Owner-cash discount on Japan share of normalized owner cash.

component_groups.label: U.S. worksite voluntary benefits engine
component_groups.economic_claim: U.S. accident, disability, and hospital indemnity worksite channel
component_groups.valuation_basis: Owner-cash discount on U.S. share of normalized owner cash.

component_groups.label: Net debt and liquidity claims
component_groups.economic_claim: Senior notes and yen debt net of cash
component_groups.valuation_basis: Net asset value on filing-locked debt less cash.

component_groups.label: Investment-mark and yen stress reserve
component_groups.economic_claim: Credit spread, JGB mark, and yen translation downside
component_groups.valuation_basis: Bounded negative reserve; not full policy liability NAV subtraction.
component_groups.adjustments: GAAP net income $3646.0M vs NII $4076.0M shows mark sensitivity.

limitations: Segment owner-cash split uses earned premium proportion; segment operating income not separately disclosed.
Yen translation and investment marks remain widest judgment bands.

component_review_queue.decision_rule: Each assigned rater records accept, challenge, or reject with a proposed range and evidence. The aggregator may not silently average disputed ranges.

items.label: Japan supplemental insurance owner-cash engine
mandatory_checks: Is the valuation method appropriate for this economic claim?
Is the security's ownership claim and economic-unit denominator reconciled correctly?
Does the evidence support the low/base/high range and its timing?
Are comparable assets truly like-for-like after production, contract, location, capital, ownership, and realization adjustments?
For an option, are success probability, time to realization, and remaining owner-funded capital explicit?
Is the component additive, or already embedded in another component?
What observation would move the base estimate materially?

valuation_method_route.label: High-return compounder
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism
valuation_method_route.decision_rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

method_route.label: High-return compounder
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism
method_route.decision_rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

required_refresh_triggers: new filing or material operating update

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

universal_valuation_contract.decision_rule: No security is decision-grade until every material claim is valued exactly once by a valid calculation proof and every material evidence blocker is resolved.

paths.label: Growth theory (pre-falsifier)
paths.weight_why: Segment blend before falsifier pass; diagnostic only when it differs from falsifier-adjusted.
weight_falsifiers: Included at full weight alongside falsifier-adjusted without divergence note

paths.label: Bear scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Downside stress; low weight because bear is a sensitivity, not a competing explanation of base mechanics.
weight_falsifiers: Bear assumptions become base case in filings (e.g. sustained volume collapse)
paths.hard_to_vary: yes: asymmetric low weight is structural

paths.label: Bull scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Upside stress probe; higher than bear because optionality archetype has skew, but still below primary paths.
weight_falsifiers: Bull growth sustained in filings for 4+ quarters without falsifier trigger

qualitative_adjustments.factor: No dated predictive attribute at spot
qualitative_adjustments.rationale: Scarcity is descriptive; no timed mispricing catalyst in base
qualitative_adjustments.falsifier: Cross-check names dated catalyst with timeline

deutsch_checks.reach: Weights encode evidence tier (A primary → D external), not fitted to hit a target IRR

synthesis.notes: Capstone IRR combining filings, segments, overlays, third-party, qualitative

context_overlay.disclaimer: Context only. Tailwinds inform stance and overlay sizing; they do not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion to base case requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

insider_signal.disclaimer: Context only. Insider activity informs scenario confidence and stance discussion; it does not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

scenario_confidence.note: Scenario weights are qualitative confidence only; Lawrence base IRR unchanged.

lens_consensus.disclaimer: Advisory lens blend only. Does not replace Lawrence total-synthesis stance gate without [HUMAN REVIEW].
